name: Meares Island Cultural Walk
location: Meares Island, Tla-o-qui-aht Tribal Park — water taxi from Tofino government dock
highlights: Guided walks with Tla-o-qui-aht knowledge keepers — traditional plant knowledge, cultural history, and relationship to the old-growth forest
The most deeply Lila practice experience on Vancouver Island — Ancient Practices as Indigenous ecological knowledge
Walking the Hanging Garden Tree cedar grove with a Tla-o-qui-aht guide transforms the experience from forest hike to living teaching
Water taxi crosses from Tofino to Meares Island; guides meet at the dock
bookingWindow: Book through Tourism Tofino or directly with Tla-o-qui-aht Tribal Parks operators
hours: By arrangement; check tourismtofino.com for current guided walk offerings

name: Hot Springs Cove — Maquinna Marine Provincial Park
location: Maquinna Marine Provincial Park — 37 km north of Tofino; boat or seaplane access only
highlights: Natural geothermal pools cascading in tiers directly into the Pacific — nowhere else quite like this
A 2-kilometer boardwalk through old-growth rainforest leads from the dock to the springs
Boat access only (zodiac or water taxi, ~90 min from Tofino) or seaplane; the journey is part of the experience
Hot pool above, cool Pacific tidal surge below — the contrast cycle is natural and raw
bookingWindow: Book boat transport through Remote Passages, Ahous Adventures, or Tofino water taxi operators
hours: Day trips only; depart Tofino early morning

name: Ancient Cedars Spa
highlights: Cedar steam, seaweed wraps using local Clayoquot Sound kelp, and treatments with botanicals foraged from the surrounding old-growth
Treatment rooms with old-growth forest and ocean views — the setting is the practice
Signature treatments include the Coastal Cedar Experience and Wild Pacific Healing Ritual
All three braids present in a single treatment: Sacred Terrain (forest materials), Ancient Practices (traditional botanicals), Elemental Encounters (Pacific elements)
bookingWindow: Book at wickinn.com — open to non-guests for treatments with availability

name: Cathedral Grove Forest Bathing
location: MacMillan Provincial Park (Cathedral Grove), Highway 4 — midway between Port Alberni and Tofino
highlights: Roadside old-growth stand of Douglas firs up to 800 years old — the most accessible old-growth on the island
Arriving here before most day trippers (before 9am) allows for genuine forest bathing in near-silence
The Threshold moment — most visitors pass through quickly; slow down, sit at the base of a fir, look up
Free and accessible; a natural pause point on the Highway 4 crossing
bookingWindow: Self-guided — pull over on Highway 4, no reservation needed
hours: Open 24 hours; BC Parks day use area

name: Kayaking Clayoquot Sound
location: Clayoquot Sound — launches from Tofino waterfront
highlights: Paddling into 350,000 hectares of islands, inlets, and old-growth shores — wilderness within 20 minutes of Tofino
Multiple operators offer guided tours; independent rental available for experienced paddlers
Grey whales, bears, and seabirds visible from the water — a completely different perspective on the Sound
Tofino Sea Kayaking Company and Remote Passages are the most established operators
bookingWindow: Book guided tours at tofino-kayaking.com or remotepassages.com
hours: Morning departures recommended; book 1–2 days ahead

name: Cold Water Immersion — Chesterman Beach
location: Chesterman Beach or Long Beach, Tofino
highlights: The Pacific at Chesterman and Long Beach runs 10–14°C year-round — a deliberate practice in any season
Walk in to chest depth, breathe through the shock, and stay for two minutes — the body adapts
Storm season (November–January) in particular: entering cold surf is a genuinely Elemental Encounter
No facilities, no instruction — just the Pacific doing what it does

name: Surfing at Tofino
location: Cox Bay and Chesterman Beach, Tofino
highlights: Cold water, real waves — surfing here is an elemental practice, not a resort amenity
Cox Bay is the beginner-friendly break; multiple surf schools offer lessons for all levels
Wetsuits (4/3mm minimum, 5/4mm in winter) and boards available from Pacific Surf School and others
Year-round surfing; storm season (Sept–Nov) brings the biggest consistent swells
bookingWindow: Book lessons at pacificsurfschool.com or walk-in for rentals
hours: Daily; surf school lessons typically morning
